_comment: Cross-mapping between grading SCALES — how to 'translate' a grade (e.g., what an 'A' means) from one scale to another. relationship: exact|partial|related|none. coverage: how much carries over (full|most|partial|minimal|none). MOST of these are institution_dependent and are NOT universal equivalences — verify on the school/district policy. Query with tools/crosswalk.py --grade. Prose: ../grade-scales.md.

entries.note: A -> 4.0 on an UNWEIGHTED 4.0 GPA. Plus/minus and weighted (honors/AP/IB) GPA change this; verify.
A 90-92% is an 'A' on a 10-point scale but a 'B' on a 7-point scale (A starts at 93). Translating an 'A' across schools is NOT 1:1 — confirm each school's cutoffs.
Level 3 = 'meets/proficient'. SBG-to-letter conversion is contested and school-defined; do not assert 3=B universally.
IB 7 is the top band but the IB-to-letter table is set by each school/college; flag for confirmation, never assert a universal 7=A.
An AP EXAM score (1-5) is NOT a course letter grade and does not translate to one. College credit for a 5 varies by college. Keep exam score and course grade separate.
